name: io.github.couchbase/mcp-server-couchbase
description: Couchbase MCP Server is a self-hosted MCP Server that allows AI agents to connect to and interact with data in Couchbase clusters, whether hosted on Capella or self-managed. It provides tools across categories including Cluster Health, Data Schema, Key-Value, Query, and Performance — with safety controls via read-only mode and fine-grained tool disabling. It supports both STDIO and Streamable HTTP transports. Couchbase MCP server is distributed as a Python Package Index (PyPI) package and via Docker. Enterprise support for Couchbase MCP Server is available by licensing Couchbase AI Data Plane, which also entitles use and enterprise support of Couchbase Agent Memory and Couchbase Agent Catalog.

environmentVariables.name: CB_CONNECTION_STRING
environmentVariables.description: Couchbase connection string. Required for connecting to the cluster.

environmentVariables.name: CB_USERNAME
environmentVariables.description: Couchbase database username. Required for basic authentication.

environmentVariables.name: CB_PASSWORD
environmentVariables.description: Couchbase database password. Required for basic authentication.

environmentVariables.name: CB_CA_CERT_PATH
environmentVariables.description: Couchbase CA certificate path. Required for TLS authentication in non Capella clusters.

environmentVariables.name: CB_CLIENT_CERT_PATH
environmentVariables.description: Couchbase client certificate path. Required for mTLS authentication.

environmentVariables.name: CB_CLIENT_KEY_PATH
environmentVariables.description: Couchbase client key path. Required for mTLS authentication.

environmentVariables.name: CB_MCP_READ_ONLY_MODE
environmentVariables.description: Couchbase read only mode. Set to true to allow disable write operations across both KV and query. KV write tools are not loaded and SQL++ queries that modify data are blocked. Set to false to allow data modification queries and tools.

environmentVariables.name: CB_MCP_TRANSPORT
environmentVariables.description: Transport mode for the server (stdio, http or sse). Default is stdio

environmentVariables.name: CB_MCP_HOST
environmentVariables.description: Host to run the MCP server on (default: 127.0.0.1). Used only for HTTP and SSE transport modes.

environmentVariables.name: CB_MCP_PORT
environmentVariables.description: Port to run the MCP server on (default: 8000). Used only for HTTP and SSE transport modes.

environmentVariables.name: CB_MCP_DISABLED_TOOLS
environmentVariables.description: Tools to disable. Accepts comma-separated tool names (e.g., 'tool_1,tool_2') or a file path containing one tool name per line.

environmentVariables.name: CB_MCP_CONFIRMATION_REQUIRED_TOOLS
environmentVariables.description: Comma-separated tool names that require user confirmation before execution. Also accepts a file path containing one tool name per line. Requires the MCP client to support elicitation.

environmentVariables.name: CB_MCP_LOG_LEVEL
environmentVariables.description: Logging level for the MCP server. One of off, debug, info, warning, error. Use 'off' to disable logging entirely. Invalid values fall back to info. Default is info.

environmentVariables.name: CB_MCP_LOG_SINKS
environmentVariables.description: Comma-separated list of log destinations. Allowed values: stderr, file. Include 'file' to write per-level log files; include 'stderr' to write to the console. Default is stderr.

environmentVariables.name: CB_MCP_LOG_FILE
environmentVariables.description: Base path for the per-level log files. One rotating file is written per level (e.g. mcp_server.info.log, mcp_server.error.log). Only used when 'file' is in CB_MCP_LOG_SINKS. Default is mcp_server.log.

environmentVariables.name: CB_MCP_LOG_MAX_BYTES
environmentVariables.description: Maximum size in bytes per per-level log file before it rotates. Default is 1048576 (1 MB).

environmentVariables.name: CB_MCP_OAUTH_JWT_JWKS_URI
environmentVariables.description: JWKS endpoint of the identity provider used to verify bearer JWT signatures. Required to enable OAuth, along with CB_MCP_OAUTH_JWT_ISSUER and CB_MCP_OAUTH_JWT_AUDIENCE. Only honored when CB_MCP_TRANSPORT=http.

environmentVariables.name: CB_MCP_OAUTH_JWT_ISSUER
environmentVariables.description: Expected JWT 'iss' claim value. Also advertised as the authorization server in Protected Resource Metadata when CB_MCP_OAUTH_MCP_BASE_URL is set. Required to enable OAuth.

environmentVariables.name: CB_MCP_OAUTH_JWT_AUDIENCE
environmentVariables.description: Expected JWT 'aud' claim value. Required to enable OAuth.

environmentVariables.name: CB_MCP_OAUTH_JWT_ALGORITHM
environmentVariables.description: JWT signing algorithm. One of RS256/384/512, ES256/384/512, PS256/384/512. Default is RS256.

environmentVariables.name: CB_MCP_OAUTH_MCP_BASE_URL
environmentVariables.description: Public base URL of this MCP server. When set, the server publishes RFC 9728 Protected Resource Metadata at <base_url>/.well-known/oauth-protected-resource/mcp so PRM-aware clients can discover the authorization server. Optional.

packageArguments.name: --connection-string
packageArguments.description: Couchbase connection string. Required for connecting to the cluster.

packageArguments.name: --username
packageArguments.description: Couchbase database username. Required for basic authentication.

packageArguments.name: --password
packageArguments.description: Couchbase database password. Required for basic authentication.

packageArguments.name: --ca-cert-path
packageArguments.description: Couchbase CA certificate path. Required for TLS authentication in non Capella clusters.

packageArguments.name: --client-cert-path
packageArguments.description: Couchbase client certificate path. Required for mTLS authentication.

packageArguments.name: --client-key-path
packageArguments.description: Couchbase client key path. Required for mTLS authentication.

packageArguments.name: --read-only-mode
packageArguments.description: Couchbase read only mode. Set to true to disable write operations across both KV and query. KV write tools are not loaded and SQL++ queries that modify data are blocked. Set to false to allow data modification queries and tools.

packageArguments.name: --transport
packageArguments.description: Transport mode for the server (stdio, http or sse). Default is stdio

packageArguments.name: --host
packageArguments.description: Host to run the MCP server on (default: 127.0.0.1). Used only for HTTP and SSE transport modes.

packageArguments.name: --port
packageArguments.description: Port to run the MCP server on (default: 8000). Used only for HTTP and SSE transport modes.

packageArguments.name: --disabled-tools
packageArguments.description: Tools to disable. Accepts comma-separated tool names (e.g., 'tool_1,tool_2') or a file path containing one tool name per line.

packageArguments.name: --confirmation-required-tools
packageArguments.description: Comma-separated tool names that require user confirmation before execution. Also accepts a file path containing one tool name per line. Requires the MCP client to support elicitation.

packageArguments.name: --log-level
packageArguments.description: Logging level for the MCP server. One of off, debug, info, warning, error. Use 'off' to disable logging entirely. Invalid values fall back to info. Default is info.

packageArguments.name: --log-sinks
packageArguments.description: Comma-separated list of log destinations. Allowed values: stderr, file. Include 'file' to write per-level log files; include 'stderr' to write to the console. Default is stderr.

packageArguments.name: --log-file
packageArguments.description: Base path for the per-level log files. One rotating file is written per level (e.g. mcp_server.info.log, mcp_server.error.log). Only used when 'file' is in --log-sinks. Default is mcp_server.log.

packageArguments.name: --log-max-bytes
packageArguments.description: Maximum size in bytes per per-level log file before it rotates. Default is 1048576 (1 MB).

packageArguments.name: --oauth-jwks-uri
packageArguments.description: JWKS endpoint of the identity provider used to verify bearer JWT signatures. Required to enable OAuth, along with --oauth-issuer and --oauth-audience. Only honored when --transport=http.

packageArguments.name: --oauth-issuer
packageArguments.description: Expected JWT 'iss' claim value. Also advertised as the authorization server in Protected Resource Metadata when --oauth-mcp-base-url is set. Required to enable OAuth.

packageArguments.name: --oauth-audience
packageArguments.description: Expected JWT 'aud' claim value. Required to enable OAuth.

packageArguments.name: --oauth-algorithm
packageArguments.description: JWT signing algorithm. One of RS256/384/512, ES256/384/512, PS256/384/512. Default is RS256.

packageArguments.name: --oauth-mcp-base-url
packageArguments.description: Public base URL of this MCP server. When set, the server publishes RFC 9728 Protected Resource Metadata at <base_url>/.well-known/oauth-protected-resource/mcp so PRM-aware clients can discover the authorization server. Optional.
